question: Q1a: Which of these issues is the most important for [COUNTRY] today?
Q1b: Which is the next most important?
Q2a: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Private enterprise is the best way to solve [COUNTRY’S] economic problems.

Q2b: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: It is the responsibility of the government to reduce the differences in income between people with high incomes and those with low incomes.
Q2c: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: [COUNTRY] should limit the import of foreign products in order to protect its national economy.
Q2d: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: [COUNTRY] should limit immigration in order to protect our national way of life.
Q2e: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: International organizations are taking away too much power from the [COUNTRY NATIONALITY] government.

Q3a: Looking at the list below, please tick a box next to the one thing you think should be [COUNTRY’S] highest priority, the most important thing it should do: [COUNTRY] should maintain order in the nation.
option_text: Give people more say in government decisions

question: Q3b: Which one do you think should be [COUNTRY’S] next highest priority, the second most important thing it should do: [COUNTRY] should maintain order in the nation.
option_text: Give people more say in government decisions

question: Q4: Generally speaking, would you say that most people can be trusted, or that you can’t be too careful in dealing with people?
Q5a: On a scale of 0 to 10, how much do you personally trust University research centres?
Q5b: On a scale of 0 to 10, how much do you personally trust The news media?
Q5c: On a scale of 0 to 10, how much do you personally trust Business and industry?
Q5d: On a scale of 0 to 10, how much do you personally trust The [COUNTRY NATIONALITY PARLIAMENT]?
Q6: Generally speaking, how concerned are you about environmental issues?
Q7: Here is a list of some different environmental problems. Which problem, if any, do you think is the most important for [COUNTRY] as a whole?
Q8: There has been a lot of discussion about the world’s climate and the idea it has been changing in recent decades. Which of the following statements comes closest to your opinion?
option_text: The world’s climate has not been changing
Has been changing mostly due to natural processes
Has been changing about equally due to natural processes and human activity
Has been changing mostly due to human activity

question: Q9a: On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad or good do you think the impacts of climate change will be for the world as a whole? (0 means extremely bad, 10 means extremely good)
Q9b: On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad or good do you think the impacts of climate change will be for [COUNTRY]? (0 means extremely bad, 10 means extremely good)
Q10a: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Modern science will solve our environmental problems with little change to our way of life.
Q10b: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: We worry too much about the future of the environment and not enough about prices and jobs today.
Q10c: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Almost everything we do in modern life harms the environment.
Q10d: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People worry too much about human progress harming the environment.
Q10e: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: In order to protect the environment [COUNTRY] needs economic growth.
Q10f: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Economic growth always harms the environment.
Q11a: How willing would you be to pay much higher prices in order to protect the environment?
Q11b: How willing would you be to pay much higher taxes in order to protect the environment?
Q11c: How willing would you be to accept cuts in your standard of living in order to protect the environment?
Q11d: How willing would you be to accept a reduction in the size of [COUNTRY’s] protected nature areas, in order to open them up for economic development?
Q12a: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: It is just too difficult for someone like me to do much about the environment.
Q12b: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: I do what is right for the environment, even when it costs more money or takes more time.
Q12c: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: There are more important things to do in life than protect the environment.
Q12d: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: There is no point in doing what I can for the environment unless others do the same.
Q12e: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Many of the claims about environmental threats are exaggerated.
Q12f: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: I find it hard to know whether the way I live is helpful or harmful to the environment.
Q12g: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: Environmental problems have a direct effect on my everyday life.
Q13a: In general, do you think that air pollution caused by cars is extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q13b: In general, do you think that air pollution caused by industry is extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q13c: In general, do you think that pesticides and chemicals used in farming are extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q13d: In general, do you think that pollution of [COUNTRY’S] rivers, lakes and streams is extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q13e: In general, do you think that a rise in the world’s temperature caused by climate change is extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q13f: In general, do you think that modifying the genes of certain crops is extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q13g: In general, do you think that nuclear power stations are extremely dangerous for the environment?
Q14a: Which of these approaches do you think would be the best way of getting business and industry in [COUNTRY] to protect the environment?
option_text: Heavy fines for businesses that damage the environment
Use the tax system to reward businesses that protect the environment
More information and education for businesses about the advantages of protecting the environment

question: Q14b: Which of these approaches do you think would be the best way of getting people and their families in [COUNTRY] to protect the environment?
option_text: Heavy fines for people who damage the environment
Use the tax system to reward people who protect the environment
More information and education for people about the advantages of protecting the environment

question: Q15: How much, if at all, do you enjoy being outside in nature?

Q16: In the last twelve months how often, if at all, have you engaged in any leisure activities outside in nature, such as hiking, bird watching, swimming, skiing, other outdoor activities or just relaxing?
Q17c: In a typical week, on how many days do you eat beef, lamb, or products that contain them?
Q19a: How often do you make a special effort to sort glass, tins, plastic, newspapers, etc. for recycling?
option_text: Recycling not available where I live

question: Q19b: How often do you avoid buying certain products for environmental reasons?
Q20: Are you a member of any group whose main aim is to preserve or protect the environment?
Q21a: In the last five years, have you signed a petition about an environmental issue?
Q21b: In the last five years, have you given money to an environmental group?
Q21c: In the last five years, have you taken part in a protest or demonstration about an environmental issue?
Q22a: Thinking about your neighbourhood, to what extent, if at all, was it affected by air pollution over the last twelve months?
Q22b: Thinking about your neighbourhood, to what extent, if at all, was it affected by water pollution over the last twelve months?

Q22c: Thinking about your neighbourhood, to what extent, if at all, was it affected by extreme weather events (such as severe storms, droughts, floods, heat waves, cold snaps, etc.) over the last twelve months?
